sutta: is 1 of the important sutas buddha said monks these 5 things ought to be often contemplated by woman and man by householder and 1 gone forth what 5 this 1 i am not the nature to age i have not got i have not gone beyond aging this ought to be often contemplated by woman and man by householder and by 1 gone forth second 1 i am not the nature to sicken i have not gone beyond sickening the third 1 i'm not the nature to die i have not gone beyond dying the fourth 1 all that is mind beloved and pleasing will become otherwise will become separated from me the fifth 1 i'm the owner of my kama heir to my kama born of my kama related to my kama abide supported by my karma whatever karma i shall do for good or for ill of that i shall be the heir thus we should frequently recollect monks to what right and or the thought i am of the nature to age i have not gone beyond aging to be often contemplated by woman and man by householder and by 1 gone forth monks beings and youth are obsessed with the pride of youth wanting in that pride they go about working evil indeed word and thought to 1 who often contemplates that aging that pride of youth to 1 who often contemplates aging that pride of youth is either got rid of altogether or reduced monks it is to this end that the thought i am of the nature to age i have not gone beyond aging to be often contemplated monks to what end or the thought i am of the nature to sicken i have not gone beyond sickening to be often contemplated monks beings in health are obsessed with the pride of health wanting in that pride they go about working evil in deed word and thought to 1 who often contemplates sickening or sickness that pride of health is either got rid of altogether or reduced monks to what end of the thought i am the of the nature to die have not gone beyond dying to be often contemplated monks beings in the fullness of life are obsessed with the pride of life wanting in that pride they go about working evil indeed word and thought to 1 who often contemplates death that pride of life is either got rid of all together or reduced monks to 1 end of the thought all that is mine beloved and pleasing will become otherwise will become separated from me to be often contemplated monks beings are obsessed with a passionate desire for those who are dear excited by that passion they go about working evil indeed word and thought to 1 who often contemplates impermanence that passionate desire is either got rid of altogether or reduced monks to what end of the thought i am the owner of my karma heir to my kama born of my karma related to my karma abide supported by my karma whatever karma i shall do for good or for ill of that i shall be the heir to be often contemplated monks the ways of beings are evil indeed evil in word and evil in thought to 1 who often contemplates i am the owner of my karma cetera those evil ways are got rid of all together or reduced monks to this end of the thought i am the owner of my karma etc to be often contemplated by woman and men by householder and 1 gone forth monks the aryan disciple reflects thus not only am i of the nature to age who have not gone beyond ageing but wheresoever there are beings coming and going fearing on and arising all out of the nature to age none have gone beyond aging and while he often contemplates this the way comes into being and that way he follows makes become and develops and in doing so the fetters are got rid of the tendencies are removed so 2 of the thoughts not only am i of the nature to sicken to die not only to me is there change and separation from those beloved and pleasing i am not the only 1 who is the owner of his karma etc and while he often contemplates these thoughts the way comes into being and that way he follows makes become and develops and in so doing the factors i got rid of the tendencies are removed that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
this is 1 of the very popular contemplations that the buddha asks monks and lay people and nuns etc to practice every day some of you might have heard the pali chan i think it goes something like jarrah jarang anatito etc [Laughter] so this 1 these uh these are very useful contemplations and the first 1 is to contemplate that you are aging day by day you are aging time is limited and this is some similarly like fish in the pond the pond is drying up there's no rain the sun is beating down on the pond day by day the water is getting less and less and the fish very soon will pass away so in the same way every day we contemplate the sense of time is running out for us day by day our we according to statistics we on the average live up to about 70 so if you calculate how old we are then we can know how much how many years you have left and that number of years means that number of christmas or chinese new year you have to you are left with to celebrate 1 goes off and year 1 less year by year so that is aging we have to contemplate second 1 is sickening as we grow all the chances of becoming sicker increase maybe today you have a you have a healthy body how would you know whether 1 year from now you'll have a healthy body there's so many people with cancer i think now the statistics tells us what 1 in 5 or something people have cancer so we have to keep this in mind and look around us how many people are there in this hall maybe 20 something out of there how many uh divide by 5 how many will get cancer so when you look at it this way and the chances you know you're getting cancer quite high so we have to consider this uh every day third 1 of the nature to die you have to remember and the graves are not uh graves of all old people there are many graves of young people graves of people in their middle age 1 of the lady arahants i forgot which 1 i don't know whether it's patachara in the theory gata she came back from pindabad arms round she had been practicing meditation been a nun for several years so she came back from pindabad arms round and she took the water as usual the monks and nuns when we come back from arms around her feet are dirty muddy sometimes so she took the water and splashed on her feet when she splashed the water on her feet she noticed the water run a little way along the ground and sink into the earth then it struck her some things rock her she repeated the second time she splashed the water on the ground she saw the water run a little bit further and sink into the ground and then she did it the third time and the water ran a bit further and sank into the ground and then it made her realize that are beings who die in their young age the beings who die in the middle age and beings who die in their old age but all have to die and from there inside arose and she attained arahanth butchara the 2 sons the 2 young sons died in the flood and the husband was killed by a poisonous snake and she walked all the way home she found her parents both were killed because the lightning struck the huge tree behind the house and the tree fell on the house so because so many people died at the same time the grief struck her so badly that she became mad she walked around without any clothes until she was she met the buddha and then became a nun then from there she cultivated and became an arahant so we have to remember any time we can pass away there's no surety that we have another 5 years or 10 years or 20 years ahead of us there are people who die of accidents people die of cancer and die in many ways so we always have to remember that don't think of enjoying life as though they have a long life ahead of us life is uncertain but death is certain is a very popular scene then the fourth 1 all that is mind beloved and pleasing will become otherwise will become separated from me all those that we love whether it's our relatives our close ones or our property material goods etc conditions will change so those that we love either they separate from us or 1 day those that we love can become our enemies also the property that we love sometimes now can be lost and so all these uh we have to remember everything is impermanent and even if they were not lost and they were they didn't leave us eventually we will have to leave them we will have to leave everything behind and go our way when death touches us on the shoulder so this is another contemplation we have to remember then the fifth 1 i'm the owner of my karma heir to my karma born of my karma related to my karma abide supported by my amma whatever karma i shall do for good or for ill of that i shall be the air you see every moment every day we are supported by our kama and whatever kanga we are doing now will determine our future life so if we understand the working of kama we will try to practice a wholesome kama and not practice unwholesome unskillful karma so these are the 5 very important contemplations

the nexuta is
aud_file: 044_Anguttara Nikaya Book 5E 5653 - 5657 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.mp3

items: I am not the nature to age, I have not gone beyond aging
I am not the nature to sicken, I have not gone beyond sickening
I'm not the nature to die, I have not gone beyond dying
All that is mine beloved and pleasing will become otherwise will become separated from me
I'm the owner of my karma, heir to my karma, born of my karma, related to my karma, abide supported by my karma
chain.category: the 5 contemplations for overcoming the 5 hindrances
